Put m=ef, s=ŽĆmodf, and╬╗=i=0ŌłæeŌłÆ1Žä╦ēiŌłłF2[╬öe,f].

The linearized Schreier relation is Ōäō=(Žā╦ēŌłÆs+╬╗)X+(1+╬╗Žā╦ē)Y.As in the prior turns, this has rank mŌłÆ1, so after pro-2 Tietze elimination Ue,f has m+2 minimal generators and one remaining relation. That is exactly the expected generator count for the maximal pro-2 Galois group of a degree-m 2-adic field; LabuteŌĆÖs local-field theorem gives n(G)=d+2 and identifies the cup product with the Hilbert symbol.Cambridge University Press & Assessment I rechecked the two cup-product blocks. On a nontrivial Žä-character block, Turn 9ŌĆÖs corrected exponent ŌłÆ2ŽĆ givesB╬▒=I+Ts+TŌłÆs.

Writing f=2af0 with f0 odd, the idempotent satisfies sŌēĪ1(mod2a) and sŌēĪ0(modf0). Hence ╬Čs has 2-power order for every f-th root ╬Č. The equation 1+╬Čs+╬ČŌłÆs=0 would force ╬Čs to have order 3, impossible. So every nontrivial tame-inertia block is nonsingular.For the trivial Žä-block, Turn 11 had the right conclusion but its displayed quotient was easy to misread. The correct even-f substitution is

Y=P(T)X+Y╦ē,P(T)=1+T1+TŌłÆs,because 1+TŌłŻ1+TŌłÆs when s is odd. The reduced X-block multiplier is h(T)=1+P(T)+P(TŌłÆ1).

For a nontrivial f-th root ╬Č,(1+╬Č)h(╬Č)=╬ČŌłÆs+╬Čs+1.

Thus h(╬Č)=0 would imply ╬Č2s+1=1. But 2s+1 is odd and congruent to 1 modulo f0, so gcd(f,2s+1)=1. Hence ╬Č=1, contradiction; and h(1)=1. Therefore the trivial block is also nonsingular.The new step is to turn Turn 11ŌĆÖs mod-4 calculation into the full dyadic orientation invariant. The Bockstein calculation gives ╬▓(Žå)=ŽåŌł¬╬║e,f,where╬║e,f(Xg)=1,╬║e,f(Yg)=1,╬║e,f (S)=0(gŌłł╬öe,f).This identifies the candidateŌĆÖs sign character, i.e. the Kummer class of ŌłÆ1. The invariant block of Ue,fab has 2-primary Smith factor 2, so q(Ue,f)=2. For the sign kernelVe,f=ker╬║e,f,the same invariant-block ReidemeisterŌĆōSchreier reduction gives Smith form diag(1,4),while all nontrivial and augmentation blocks have 2-adic unit determinant after the above reductions. Thus Ve,fab has C4 -torsion and no C8 -torsion. LabuteŌĆÖs classification says the q-invariant is the highest 2-power detected by the image of the orientation character, and two Demushkin groups with the same rank and orientation image are isomorphic.Cambridge University Press & Assessment+1

Consequently the image of the orientation character for Ue,f is not merely nontrivial modulo 4; it is exactly {┬▒1}├ŚU2(2)=U2(1).

So for every finite tame quotient (e,f), the candidateŌĆÖs Ue,f is an abstract Demushkin group withn(Ue,f)=ef+2,q(Ue,f)=2,ImŽć=U2(1).This matches the maximal pro-2 Galois group of the corresponding tame finite extension L/Q2, since such L has degree ef, contains ╬╝2 but not ╬╝4, and has the same cyclotomic intersection with the 2-power cyclotomic tower. LabuteŌĆÖs local-field section gives precisely that local maximal pro-p groups are Demushkin, with H1 identified with K\*/K \*p, cup product the Hilbert symbol, and the q=2 case governed by the orientation image.Cambridge University Press & Assessment What this still does not prove is the final profinite presentation of GQ2. The remaining gap is now narrower and more structural: one must show that these finite-tame Demushkin identifications are compatible with the ╬öe,f -action, or equivalently supply the dyadic replacement for the JannsenŌĆōWingberg formation step. Abstractly, every finite tame pro-2 kernel now has the right Demushkin type and full orientation image. What is not yet written is the theorem saying that this compatible family of oriented Demushkin kernels forces the whole profinite extension over the tame quotient to be the absolute Galois group.

ESTABLISHED: The Turn 9ŌĆō11 candidate survives the full finite-tame abstract pro-2 test. For every (e,f), the induced Ue,f is a Demushkin group with rank ef+2, nondegenerate cup product, q=2, and full orientation image U2(1)={┬▒1}├ŚU2(2). The sign kernel has C4, not C8, so the missing orientation invariant from earlier turns is now pinned down.

GAP: This is still not a complete presentation proof. The remaining missing ingredient is a formal ╬öe,f -equivariant formation/uniqueness argument showing that the compatible system of these oriented Demushkin kernels determines the full absolute Galois group over the tame quotient.

NEXT: Prove or quote the required dyadic formation lemma: a profinite group over the tame quotient with these ╬ö-operator Demushkin kernels, orientation character, and Kummer H1 -module is uniquely isomorphic to GQ2.[BREAKTHROUGH]
